---
title: "Let's Talk Machine Learning Models" 
subtitle: |
  Weighing Pros & Cons of Fit & Run Time
# image: 
categories: []
date: 2026-03-21
draft: true
# citation:
  # url: 
execute:
    message: false
    warning: false
params:
  slug: skore-model-comparisons
  date: 2025-08-25
---

[Dataset Link](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Ld9E_5Da9xaZ31VxLCQ41UG26FWjNXHBuU6YHqq1kaY/edit?gid=0#gid=0)

```{python}
#| label: loading in data
#| eval: true
#| echo: true

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import plotnine as pn
import joblib
from pyhere import here
from janitor import clean_names

# from sklearn.pipeline import make_pipeline, Pipeline
# from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler, OneHotEncoder, OneHotEncoder
# from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
# from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer
# from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split, GridSearchCV, RandomizedSearchCV
# from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression, SGDRegressor
# from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeRegressor
# from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestRegressor
# from sklearn.metrics import mean_absolute_error, mean_squared_error, root_mean_squared_error, r2_score
# from sklearn.inspection import permutation_importance

# train/test split
from sklearn.model_selection import TimeSeriesSplit
from skore import compare, evaluate

jpcolor = 'seagreen'

pd.set_option('display.max_columns', None)
pd.options.mode.copy_on_write = True

df = pd.read_csv(here('posts/2026-03-21-skore/american_airlines_forecasting.csv')).clean_names(case_type = 'snake')
```

```{python}
train = df.iloc[0:round(df.shape[0] * .8), :]
test = df.iloc[round(df.shape[0] * .8):, :]

print(df.shape)
print(train.shape)
print(test.shape)
```

```{python}
y_train = df['close']
x_train = df['date']

ts_cv = TimeSeriesSplit(n_splits = 5, gap = 30)
train_splits = list(ts_cv.split(x_train, y_train))

# train1, test1 = all_splits[0]
# train2, test2 = all_splits[1]
# train3, test3 = all_splits[2]
# train4, test4 = all_splits[3]
# train5, test5 = all_splits[4]

# x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(x, y, test_size = .2, random_state = 12345)
# x_train_sub, x_val, y_train_sub, y_val = train_test_split(x_train, y_train, test_size = .2, random_state = 12345)
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

print('Full dataset:', df.shape)

# print('Full training features:', x_train.shape)
# print('Full training outcome:', y_train.shape)

# print('Training set features:', x_train_sub.shape)
# print('Training set outcome:', y_train_sub.shape)

# print('Validation set features:', x_val.shape)
# print('Validation set outcome:', y_val.shape)

# print('Test set features:', x_test.shape)
# print('Test set outcome:', y_test.shape)
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

# for visualizations
# train = pd.concat([x_train, y_train], axis = 1)
# train.head()
```

# Exploratory Data Analysis

```{python}
pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(train, pn.aes('date', 'close'))
  + pn.geom_line(color = jpcolor, group = 1)
  + pn.theme_light()
)
```

```{python}
# num_features = x_train.select_dtypes(int).columns.tolist()
# cat_features = x_train.select_dtypes(object).columns.tolist()
```

## Visualizations

```{python}
#| eval: false

[pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(train, pn.aes(i, 'exam_score'))
  + pn.geom_point(alpha = .3, color = jpcolor)
  + pn.geom_smooth(method = 'lm')
  + pn.theme_light()
) for i in num_features]
```

From this we can see that there are linear relationships; however, we can also see there are outliers in many of these scatterplots. Let's see if we could dive in a little deeper and uncover potential interactions based on categorical features.

```{python}
#| eval: false

[pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(train, pn.aes('hours_studied', 'exam_score', color = i))
  + pn.geom_point(alpha = .5)
  + pn.geom_smooth(method = 'lm')
  + pn.theme_light()
) for i in cat_features]
```

```{python}
#| eval: false

[pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(train, pn.aes('attendance', 'exam_score', color = i))
  + pn.geom_point(alpha = .5)
  + pn.geom_smooth(method = 'lm')
  + pn.theme_light()
) for i in cat_features]
```

Based on those plots, it seems like there are no interactions between hours studied and attendance with the categorical indicators for exam scores.

```{python}
#| eval: false

[pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(train, pn.aes('tutoring_sessions', 'exam_score', color = i))
  + pn.geom_point(alpha = .5)
  + pn.geom_smooth(method = 'lm')
  + pn.theme_light()
) for i in cat_features]
```

# Preprocessing

```{python}
num_pipeline = make_pipeline(
    SimpleImputer(strategy = 'mean'), 
    StandardScaler()
)

cat_pipeline = make_pipeline(
    SimpleImputer(strategy = 'most_frequent'), 
    OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown = 'ignore')
)

preprocess = ColumnTransformer(transformers = [('num_trans', num_pipeline, num_features),
                                               ('cat_trans', cat_pipeline, cat_features)],
                                               remainder = 'passthrough')

linear_pipe = make_pipeline(preprocess, LinearRegression())
sgd_pipe = make_pipeline(preprocess, SGDRegressor())
tree_pipe = make_pipeline(preprocess, DecisionTreeRegressor())
random_pipe = make_pipeline(preprocess, RandomForestRegressor())
```

```{python}
preprocess.fit_transform(x_train_sub)
```

# Modeling

## Linear Regression

```{python}
linear_model = linear_pipe.fit(X = x_train_sub, y = y_train_sub)
linear_pred = linear_pipe.predict(x_val)

# print(round(mean_absolute_error(y_val, linear_pred), 2)) #MAE
# print(round(mean_squared_error(y_val, linear_pred), 2)) #MSE
print(round(root_mean_squared_error(y_val, linear_pred), 2)) #RMSE
```

## SGD Regression

```{python}
sgd_model = sgd_pipe.fit(X = x_train_sub, y = y_train_sub)
sgd_pred = sgd_pipe.predict(x_val)

# print(round(mean_absolute_error(y_val, sgd_pred), 2)) #MAE
# print(round(mean_squared_error(y_val, sgd_pred), 2)) #MSE
print(round(root_mean_squared_error(y_val, sgd_pred), 2)) #RMSE
```

## Decision Tree

```{python}
tree_model = tree_pipe.fit(X = x_train_sub, y = y_train_sub)
tree_pred = tree_pipe.predict(x_val)

# print(round(mean_absolute_error(y_val, tree_pred), 2)) #MAE
# print(round(mean_squared_error(y_val, tree_pred), 2)) #MSE
print(round(root_mean_squared_error(y_val, tree_pred), 2)) #RMSE
```

## Random Forest

```{python}
random_model = random_pipe.fit(X = x_train_sub, y = y_train_sub)
random_pred = random_pipe.predict(x_val)

# print(round(mean_absolute_error(y_val, random_pred), 2)) #MAE
# print(round(mean_squared_error(y_val, random_pred), 2)) #MSE
print(round(root_mean_squared_error(y_val, random_pred), 2)) #RMSE
```

```{python}
linear_report = evaluate(linear_model, x_val, y_val, splitter = 'prefit')
sgd_report = evaluate(sgd_model, x_val, y_val, splitter = 'prefit')
tree_report = evaluate(tree_model, x_val, y_val, splitter = 'prefit')
random_report = evaluate(random_model, x_val, y_val, splitter = 'prefit')
```

# Let's Compare Models

```{python}
comparison = compare(
    {'Linear': linear_report,
     'SGD': sgd_report,
     'Tree': tree_report,
     'Random': random_report
    }
)

comparison_metrics = comparison.metrics.summarize()
```

```{python}
compare_long = (
  comparison_metrics
  .frame()
  .reset_index()
  .clean_names(case_type = 'snake')
  .melt(id_vars = 'metric')
)

pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(compare_long.dropna(), pn.aes('Estimator', 'value'))
  + pn.geom_col(fill = jpcolor)
  + pn.facet_wrap('metric',
                  scales = 'free',
                  ncol = 1)
  + pn.theme_light()
)
```

```{python}
fit_to_time = (
  compare_long.loc[compare_long['metric']
  .isin(['RMSE', 'Predict time (s)'])]
  .pivot(index = 'Estimator',
         columns = 'metric',
         values = 'value')
  .clean_names(case_type = 'snake')
)

pn.ggplot.show(
  pn.ggplot(fit_to_time.reset_index(),
            pn.aes('predict_time_s_',
                   'rmse'))
  + pn.geom_point(pn.aes(color = 'Estimator'),
                  position = pn.position_jitter())
  + pn.theme_light()
)
```
